preamble: The phonetic transformation from the diphthong /au/ to /oi/ is an attested sound change observed in various language families, notably in Romance and Germanic languages. This shift involves a complex rearticulation of the diphthong's components, moving from a back, open-to-close configuration to a more fronted and often rounded-to-unrounded trajectory.
phoneticEffects: This shift involves the raising and rounding of the initial vowel element (from an open central/back unrounded vowel like [a] to a mid back rounded vowel like [o] or [ɔ]), coupled with the fronting and unrounding of the second vowel element (from a close back rounded vowel like [u] to a close front unrounded vowel like [i] or [ɪ])

examples.note: Umlaut of /au/, pronounced as [ɔʏ̯] or [ɔɪ̯]. E.g., 'Baum' (tree) -> 'Bäume' (trees).
